steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Runtime Sdk Version · Theory Runtime Sdk Version
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 8335 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to enforce pinned Node, TypeScript, AWS SDK v3, modular client, runtime validation, and service-error narrowing; keep every external value unknown until a runtime validator accepts it; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Pin Node 24.18.0, the TypeScript 7.0.2 native CLI, the @typescript/typescript6 package 6.0.2 whose compiler reports 6.0.3, and @aws-sdk/client-s3, lib-storage, s3-request-presigner, and cloudfront-signer 3.1087.0 as one reviewed compatibility contract.
